rationale: A cmd.exe process (PID 2936) running as corp\Administrator at High integrity level orchestrated four sequential curl.exe invocations (A0, EV-002, EV-004, EV-006) to download the same x64 DLL from a raw GitHub commit URL using -k (TLS verification bypass), depositing copies to multiple writable locations: C:\Users\Public\Music\ (EV-001, EV-003), C:\ProgramData\ (EV-005), and the Administrator's Temp directory (EV-007). The redundant multi-destination download pattern is a classic staging/persistence resilience technique — attempting multiple drop paths to ensure at least one succeeds — and the use of -k to suppress certificate errors is a common evasion tactic. The DLL payload is confirmed written to disk across three distinct locations (EV-001/EV-003, EV-005, EV-007); however, no subsequent DLL load, rundll32, regsvr32, or persistence mechanism event is present in this package, so execution of the payload is suspected but not yet confirmed. Immediate investigation is warranted to determine the DLL's content and whether it has been loaded.
